docs.title: Data-Continuum
docs.text:
Welcome to the Data-Continuum documentation.

Data-Continuum is a production-grade data pipeline sandbox designed to simulate a high-velocity Smart Logistics environment. It demonstrates the seamless flow of data from diverse sources (SQL and NoSQL) through an orchestration layer into a unified API and Machine Learning service, all while maintained under a rigorous observability stack.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Polyglot Persistence: Combines relational metadata (PostgreSQL) with high-frequency telemetry (MongoDB).
  • Event-Driven Orchestration: Powered by Apache Airflow.
  • Unified Extraction: A FastAPI layer to combine SQL and NoSQL data into a single unified state.
  • ML Integration: Integrated MLflow tracking with background training capabilities.
  • Observability: Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboards out-of-the-box.

Get started by exploring the Architecture.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The Data-Continuum architecture is built on a Polyglot Persistence pattern with an Event-Driven Orchestration layer.

docs.title: Architecture Diagram
docs.text:
graph TD
    subgraph Observability
        Prometheus[Prometheus]
        Grafana[Grafana]
    end

    subgraph Data_Layer [Data Layer]
        DB[(PostgreSQL)]
        Mongo[(MongoDB)]
        Redis[(Redis)]
    end

    subgraph Workflow_Orchestration [Workflow & Orchestration]
        AirflowWeb[Airflow Webserver]
        AirflowSched[Airflow Scheduler]
    end

    subgraph Service_ML_Layer [Service & ML Layer]
        API[API Service]
        ML[ML Service]
        MLFlow[MLFlow Tracking]
        Seeder[Seeder]
        UI[React UI Dashboard]
    end

    UI --> API

    API --> DB
    API --> Mongo
    Seeder --> DB
    Seeder --> Mongo

    AirflowWeb --> DB
    AirflowSched --> DB
    AirflowSched --> Redis

    ML --> MLFlow

    Grafana --> Prometheus

docs.title: 1. Data Ingestion Layer (The Source)
docs.text:
• Relational Storage (SQL): PostgreSQL 15+ storing transactional shipment data (IDs, Customer info, Status, timestamps).
  • Non-Relational Storage (NoSQL): MongoDB storing high-frequency vehicle telemetry (GPS coordinates, engine sensors, fuel levels).

docs.title: 2. Orchestration & Engineering Layer (The Brain)
docs.text:
• Workflow Engine: Apache Airflow.
  • Core Responsibilities:
    • Monitor database health sensors.
    • Trigger periodic PySpark jobs for data cleaning.
    • Handle retry logic for failed extraction tasks.

docs.title: 3. Service & ML Layer (The Interface)
docs.text:
• Unified Extraction API: FastAPI service to retrieve "Unified Shipment State".
  • ML Training Service: FastAPI endpoint to trigger training of a Predictive ETA model.
  • Experiment Tracking: MLflow integration to log model versions and accuracy metrics.

docs.title: 4. Observability & UI Layer (The Monitor)
docs.text:
• Metrics: Prometheus scraping API and DB performance.
  • Visualization: Grafana dashboards for system health.

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text: The entire stack is containerized using Docker and Docker Compose, connected via an isolated internal bridge network for data services, with exposed gateways for the API/UI.

docs.title: Observability
docs.text: The Data-Continuum observability stack provides deep insights into the health, performance, and stability of the microservices.

docs.title: Prometheus
docs.text: Prometheus is configured to scrape metrics from: - FastAPI Unified Extraction API (api:8000/metrics) - FastAPI ML Service (ml:8001/metrics)

docs.title: Grafana
docs.text: Grafana connects directly to Prometheus to visualize system health, request latency, and HTTP response codes across the services.

docs.title: Accessing the UIs
docs.text:
• Prometheus: http://localhost:9090
  • Grafana: http://localhost:3000 (Default credentials: admin/admin)

docs.title: Orchestration
docs.text:
Data-Continuum uses Apache Airflow as its central orchestration engine.

Airflow is responsible for scheduling, monitoring, and triggering the various data pipelines and training services within the ecosystem.

docs.title: data_continuum_pipeline
docs.text: A daily scheduled pipeline that performs the following tasks: 1. Verify Connections: Ensures PostgreSQL and MongoDB are accessible. 2. Run PySpark Cleaning: Simulates a spark-submit job to clean and join data across the polyglot databases. 3. Trigger ML Training: Sends an HTTP request to the ML Service (http://api:8001/train) to automatically start a new round of predictive ETA training.

docs.title: Kubernetes Deployment Guide
docs.text: This project supports deployment to Kubernetes using Helm.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Kubernetes cluster (Minikube, KIND, or a cloud provider)
  • Helm installed
  • kubectl installed

docs.title: Chart Structure
docs.text: The Helm chart is located in deploy/kubernetes/helm/data-continuum. It manages: - API: FastAPI backend - UI: React frontend - ML: Machine Learning service - Seeder: Data seeding job - Infrastructure: Postgres, MongoDB, Redis (basic deployments) - Airflow: Webserver and Scheduler

docs.title: 1. Build Docker Images
docs.text:
Before deploying, ensure your Docker images are built and available in your cluster's registry.

docker build -t data-continuum-api:latest ./api
docker build -t data-continuum-ui:latest ./ui
docker build -t data-continuum-ml:latest ./ml
docker build -t data-continuum-seeder:latest ./seeder

Note: If using Minikube, run eval $(minikube docker-env) before building.

docs.title: 2. Install the Chart
docs.text:
Navigate to the root directory and run:

helm install data-continuum ./deploy/kubernetes/helm/data-continuum

docs.title: 3. Verify Deployment
docs.text:
Check the status of your pods:

kubectl get pods

docs.title: 4. Access the Services
docs.text:
You can port-forward to access the services locally:

# API
kubectl port-forward svc/data-continuum-api 8000:8000

# UI
kubectl port-forward svc/data-continuum-ui 5173:5173

# Airflow
kubectl port-forward svc/data-continuum-airflow-webserver 8080:8080

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
You can customize the deployment by modifying values.yaml or by passing --set flags during installation.

Example: 

helm install data-continuum ./deploy/kubernetes/helm/data-continuum \
  --set replicaCount=3 \
  --set api.image.tag=v1.2.3

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
To remove the deployment:

helm uninstall data-continuum

docs.title: Process-based Deployment
docs.text: This guide explains how to run Data-Continuum services as standalone OS processes. This is the simplest way to run the project without Docker or Kubernetes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.12+
  • Node.js
  • Local instances of PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and Redis.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Install Python Dependencies: 

    uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # Or .\.venv\Scripts\activate on Windows
uv pip install -r api/requirements.txt
uv pip install -r ml/requirements.txt

  2. Install UI Dependencies: 

    cd ui
npm install
cd ..

docs.title: Using Helper Scripts (Windows)
docs.text:
If you are on Windows, you can use the PowerShell scripts in the scripts/ directory:

.\scripts\start_all.ps1

docs.title: 1. Start the API
docs.text:
export POSTGRES_URL="postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/continuum"
export MONGO_URL="mongodb://admin:admin@localhost:27017/"
cd api
uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000

docs.title: 2. Start the ML Service
docs.text:
export MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI="http://localhost:5000"
cd ml
uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8001

docs.title: 3. Start the UI
docs.text:
cd ui
npm run dev

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Services use environment variables for configuration. You can also create a .env file in the root directory, though the current implementation primarily relies on direct environment variables or defaults in the code.

docs.title: Unified Extraction API
docs.text: The Unified Extraction API is built using FastAPI and serves as the single point of truth to retrieve the "Unified Shipment State".

docs.title: GET /shipments/{shipment_id}/unified
docs.text: This endpoint queries both the relational PostgreSQL database for shipment metadata and the NoSQL MongoDB database for the latest telemetry payload. It combines them into a single UnifiedState Pydantic model response.

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text: Standard health check endpoint to ensure the service is running.

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text: The API exposes standard Prometheus metrics out-of-the-box via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator.

docs.title: Ingestion Service
docs.text:
The Ingestion (or Seeding) service is responsible for simulating the high-velocity Smart Logistics environment.

It is a Python-based container that utilizes Faker, SQLAlchemy, and PyMongo to generate realistic mock data across both the SQL and NoSQL databases upon initialization.

docs.title: Process
docs.text:
1. PostgreSQL Seeding: Connects via SQLAlchemy and inserts 1,000+ Shipment records containing customer names, emails, and shipment statuses.
  2. MongoDB Seeding: Connects via PyMongo and inserts high-frequency telemetry payload mimicking GPS coordinates, engine temperatures, and fuel levels.

This ensures the continuum has an immediate flow of data for downstream services to consume and orchestrate.

docs.title: ML Training Service
docs.text: The ML Training Service is a FastAPI application dedicated to Machine Learning orchestration and model training.

docs.title: POST /train
docs.text: Triggers a background task to train a Predictive ETA model (using Scikit-Learn). It simulates feature extraction from the combined SQL/NoSQL stores.

docs.title: MLflow Integration
docs.text: The service integrates natively with an MLflow tracking server to log experiment runs, model versions, and accuracy metrics (e.g., R2 Score). All metrics are stored and viewable in the MLflow UI (http://localhost:5000).

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text: The service exposes standard Prometheus metrics out-of-the-box via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator.
